headline: Dow and Nasdaq plunge 2% as Amazon data centres hit in Iran war
summary: 10.32am: Iran is 'not just oil any more' as AWS damage moves dial US stocks have extended their slide, with selling accelerating asthe first hour of trading ticks past. The Dow is now down 1,244 points or 2.5% at 47,650, while its descent has been overtaken by the Nasdaq, which has plunged...

headline: Missile ‘megatrend’ still underestimated despite Iran conflict, says Citi
summary: Recent events in the Middle East have only reinforced an armaments megatrend, analysts at Citi believe, arguing growth expectations across the sector still look too conservative. The bank flagged RTX Corp (NYSE:RTX, XETRA:5UR), L3Harris Technologies Inc (NYSE:LHX, XETRA:HRS), Lockheed Martin...

headline: Defense companies poised to ramp up production amid Iran war
summary: Israel's airstrikes against Iran and Lebanon have Wall Street investors anxious over the risk these escalations could have on global markets if they continue for too long, as not only US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are seeing losses in Tuesday's session, but the defense industry as well. Gabelli Funds portfolio manager Tony Bancroft comes on Market Catalysts to discuss the defense manufacturer's inventory-production dynamic as Middle East conflicts ramp up and how he is forecasting increased capex spending for these contractors. Bancroft manages the Gabelli Commercial Aerospace and Defense ETF (GCAD). To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Will the U.S. Run Out of Missiles? Lockheed Martin and More Stocks Trying to Prevent It.
summary: A lot of missiles are being launched—and intercepted—in the ongoing conflict between the U.S. and Iran.  Shares of Lockheed Martin, RTX, and Boeing could benefit.  Running out of certain critical missile technologies is a possibility.

headline: There Are Worries the U.S. Could Run Out of Missiles in Iran. These Contractors Are Who It’s Turning To.
summary: A lot of missiles are being launched—and intercepted—in the ongoing conflict between the U.S. and Iran.  Running out of certain critical missile technologies is a possibility.  “Manufacturers just can’t make them fast enough,” said Vertical Research Partners analyst Rob Stallard.

headline: Tomahawk Economics: Missile Strikes Ignite Defense ETF Rally
summary: Missile strikes and rising global tensions are lifting defense ETFs. Replenishment cycles and a $1.5 tn defense budget could fuel a longer-term rally.

headline: Iran, Dip-Buyers & a Rare Buffett Stumble: Wall Street Shakes Off Its Worst Fears
summary: U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran rattled markets at the open, but seasoned dip-buyers pushed the major indices back to near-flat by the close. Meanwhile, Berkshire Hathaway's post-Buffett era gets off to a rocky start, and energy and defense stocks surge.
